intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Pyrus piranga." However, I found information on Pyrus communis, also known as the common pear, and Pyrus communis var pirinensis, also known as the Bulgarian pear, but I found information on a plant that may be referred to as "Pyrus piranga" - Pyrus calleryana,  also known as the Callery pear.

Here is a paragraph about Pyrus calleryana:

Pyrus calleryana, also known as the Callery pear, is a deciduous ornamental tree native to Asia, prized for its showy white flowers and attractive foliage. The health benefits of Pyrus calleryana are largely anecdotal, but it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus calleryana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus calleryana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
